title: Battery Input Open
symptoms: No current draw, dead phone, no USB response
repair_steps: Confirm battery voltage is above 3.7V
Inspect battery connector for lifted or burnt pins
Measure PP_BATT_VCC in diode mode
Replace damaged connector or flex
Retest DC power supply boot pattern

title: VCC_MAIN Hard Short
symptoms: Immediate high current draw, board heats instantly, no boot
repair_steps: Inject 1V into PP_VCC_MAIN with current limit
Locate heat source with thermal camera or IPA
Remove shorted capacitor or failed IC
Verify resistance and diode mode recover
Retest normal boot sequence

title: Battery Sense Line Fault
symptoms: Boot loop, incorrect battery percentage, unstable charging
repair_steps: Inspect battery flex and connector seating
Check battery sense line continuity
Compare diode reading with known-good board
Replace battery if line value is unstable
Repair damaged filter or trace

title: USB Input Path Fault
symptoms: No charging, no current draw from charger, intermittent USB detection
repair_steps: Test with known-good cable and charger
Inspect USB-C port for corrosion or bent pins
Measure VBUS path in diode mode
Replace damaged port or protection array
Verify stable input on reconnect

title: Power Key Trigger Missing
symptoms: No reaction to power key, only trickle current on charger
repair_steps: Check power button line while pressing key
Verify continuity to PMIC trigger pin
Measure diode mode on PMU_ONOFF_L
Repair open trace or connector issue
Replace damaged power button flex if needed

title: 1V8 Always Rail Missing
symptoms: No boot sequence, no low-power logic initialization
repair_steps: Check rail for short to ground
Verify PMIC input and enable condition
Inspect nearby capacitors and coil
Replace shorted capacitor or PMIC if required
Confirm stable 1.8V output

title: 3V0 Always Rail Missing
symptoms: Dead board, no normal wake sequence, accessory detection failure
repair_steps: Measure rail resistance and diode mode
Inspect PMIC output components
Check for shorted sensor or capacitor on line
Replace failed passive or PMIC output part
Verify stable 3.0V output

title: USB-C CC Negotiation Failure
symptoms: Stays at 5V only, no fast charge, weak startup current pattern
repair_steps: Measure CC1 and CC2 in diode mode
Inspect USB-C port center tongue and contacts
Check PD controller rails and data lines
Replace damaged PD controller or ESD array
Confirm correct charger negotiation

title: RTC Clock Missing
symptoms: Delayed or unstable startup, repeated restart before deeper boot stages
repair_steps: Inspect RTC crystal and surrounding passives
Check liquid damage around clock area
Compare diode reading with known-good board
Replace faulty crystal or capacitor
Retest boot progression

title: SoC Reset Line Stuck
symptoms: Low pulsing current, no image, no USB enumeration
repair_steps: Check reset line for short or leakage
Inspect SoC neighborhood for corrosion
Compare diode value with known-good board
Replace damaged pull-up or filter if needed
Retest boot current profile

title: NAND 1V8 Rail Instability
symptoms: Early boot loop, restore errors, current ramp then drop
repair_steps: Measure NAND rail in diode mode
Inspect rail capacitors and inductor
Check for heat near storage area
Replace shorted capacitor if found
Retest boot and restore

title: SDRAM 0V9 Rail Missing
symptoms: Current rises briefly and resets, no logo, no vibration
repair_steps: Measure SDRAM rail resistance and diode mode
Check PMIC buck output section
Inspect AP and RAM area for flex damage
Replace shorted cap or damaged inductor path
Verify sustained 0.9V during boot

title: CPU Core Rail Fault
symptoms: Stuck at early current plateau, brief SoC heat, no boot
repair_steps: Check for low resistance on CPU core rail
Inspect coil and caps on output side
Compare diode reading with known-good board
Remove shorted external part if present
Escalate to AP rework only after rail path is verified

title: SYS_ALIVE Assertion Failure
symptoms: Modern boards start then collapse before logo, repeated low-current cycling
repair_steps: Check SYS_ALIVE continuity between PMIC and SoC
Measure diode value against same-model donor board
Inspect handshake pull-up network
Repair damaged resistor array or filter
Retest for stable boot progression

title: AOP_NMI_POWER Fault
symptoms: Random restart, no full boot, wake and sensor anomalies on newer models
repair_steps: Check line for leakage or partial short
Inspect always-on domain passives
Compare diode reading with known-good board
Repair damaged trace, filter, or resistor network
Retest stable progression after pre-boot handshake

title: NAND Data Line Open
symptoms: Mid-boot loop, restore errors, panic after storage initialization
repair_steps: Inspect NAND solder joints under microscope
Check series resistors and filters on data lines
Measure diode values on suspect nets
Reball NAND if continuity is lost
Retest restore and normal boot

title: Boot Read Rail Failure
symptoms: Current reaches threshold then resets, no logo, DFU may still work
repair_steps: Verify PP1V2_S2 is present and stable
Check buck output coil and caps
Inspect rail filter network
Replace unstable passives
Retest restore and boot

title: Sensor EEPROM Rail Fault
symptoms: Inconsistent boot, sensor failures, calibration read issues
repair_steps: Measure PP1V8_IO for short or sag
Inspect EEPROM area for corrosion
Check data line pull-ups
Replace shorted EEPROM or damaged filter
Verify communication after repair

title: Baseband Pre-Init Rail Fault
symptoms: No modem bring-up, restore stalls, abnormal stage 5 current pattern
repair_steps: Check PP1V0_BB resistance and diode value
Inspect baseband PMU output stage
Replace shorted cap on BB rail
Reflow or reball BB PMU if necessary
Retest modem detection

title: Display AVDDH Rail Fault
symptoms: Phone boots with sound or vibration but no image
repair_steps: Confirm board boots using sound or USB detection
Measure display boost rail live voltage and diode mode
Inspect display connector for damage
Replace failed display power IC or cap
Retest with known-good screen

title: Backlight Rail Open
symptoms: Faint image only, no backlight, normal boot behavior otherwise
repair_steps: Check image with flashlight
Measure backlight output and boost coil continuity
Inspect display connector anode path
Replace backlight IC, diode, or coil
Retest full display brightness

title: MIPI Clock Pair Fault
symptoms: No image, intermittent lines, board fully boots
repair_steps: Inspect MIPI filters and connector pins
Measure pair symmetry in diode mode
Check for dropped pads or cracked filter array
Replace damaged MIPI filter
Retest with known-good display

title: Touch Rail Missing
symptoms: Display works but touch is dead or ghosting
repair_steps: Check touch rail diode value and live voltage
Inspect touch connector and filter network
Swap known-good display to isolate panel fault
Replace missing rail source or damaged filter
Verify full touch function

title: Baseband Handshake Fault
symptoms: Logo hang, no service after boot, restore modem failures
repair_steps: Measure handshake line in diode mode
Inspect resistor network between BB and AP
Check for corrosion under modem shield
Replace damaged filter or resistor pack
Retest boot and network state

title: RF Rail Short
symptoms: No signal, modem panic, heat near RF section during boot
repair_steps: Check PP1V8_RF for low resistance
Inject low voltage and locate heating part
Remove shorted capacitor if present
Replace failed RF IC if short remains internal
Retest network functionality

title: eSIM Rail Fault
symptoms: Activation issues, no provisioning, modem-related restart
repair_steps: Inspect secure element power rail
Check diode mode on power and data lines
Inspect for corrosion around eSIM area
Replace shorted passive or damaged secure element
Retest activation and carrier profile loading

title: Modem Clock Request Fault
symptoms: No service, panic logs reference modem bring-up, delayed boot
repair_steps: Check clock request line for open or leakage
Inspect nearby clock buffer passives
Compare diode reading with same-model donor
Repair damaged resistor or filter path
Retest baseband availability

title: Wi-Fi Rail Fault
symptoms: Wi-Fi grayed out, long boot, wireless panic
repair_steps: Check Wi-Fi rail for short or low diode value
Inspect wireless area for corrosion or impact
Replace shorted capacitor if found
Rework combo IC if rail remains loaded
Retest Wi-Fi and Bluetooth

title: NFC Rail Fault
symptoms: Tap to pay unavailable, slow boot, accessory authentication issues
repair_steps: Measure NFC rail and inspect filter network
Check board-to-board contact path if applicable
Compare diode reading with known-good board
Replace damaged NFC controller or passive
Retest payment and tag detection

title: UWB Rail Fault
symptoms: Precision finding unavailable, nearby device features unstable
repair_steps: Inspect UWB area for liquid damage
Measure PP1V8_UWB in diode mode
Check support lines around UWB block
Replace failed capacitor or UWB IC
Retest proximity features

title: USB-C PD 3.1 Negotiation Fault
symptoms: Slow charging, unstable dock behavior, no proper high-voltage contract
repair_steps: Use PD analyzer to confirm contract behavior
Measure PD sense and CC path diode values
Inspect port, ESD array, and controller rails
Replace failing PD controller or protection array
Verify stable PD 3.1 negotiation

title: Audio Codec Rail Fault
symptoms: No audio, voice memo failure, audio subsystem crash
repair_steps: Measure codec rail voltage and diode mode
Inspect codec area for bend damage
Check clock and reset support lines
Reflow or replace codec if power is normal
Retest recording and playback

title: Mic Bias Rail Short
symptoms: Microphone dead, Siri failure, audio init errors
repair_steps: Check mic bias line for leakage to ground
Inspect connector-side filters and ESD parts
Replace shorted passive on bias rail
Test all microphone paths
Confirm audio panic is gone

title: Speaker Boost Fault
symptoms: No loudspeaker, distorted startup sounds, amplifier overheating
repair_steps: Measure boost rail and inspect output caps
Check speaker lines for short to ground
Swap known-good speaker to isolate board fault
Replace audio amplifier if boost is missing
Retest speaker output

title: Bottom Mic Data Open
symptoms: No voice input, intermittent accessory warnings due to flex damage
repair_steps: Inspect charging flex connector and bottom mic path
Measure line in diode mode for open condition
Repair torn pad or replace connector
Test audio recording
Verify call microphone operation

title: TrueDepth Rail Fault
symptoms: Face ID unavailable, front camera init delay, normal basic boot
repair_steps: Measure TrueDepth rail and compare with known-good
Inspect front sensor flex and connector
Check for liquid damage in upper connector area
Repair missing rail source or passive fault
Retest camera and Face ID

title: Rear Camera AVDD Short
symptoms: Camera crash, slow boot, no rear camera detection
repair_steps: Disconnect camera modules and compare rail behavior
Check AVDD rail in diode mode
Determine module-side or board-side short
Replace shorted cap or camera power IC
Retest all rear cameras

title: Sensor Hub Rail Instability
symptoms: Auto-brightness, compass, or motion issues with delayed boot
repair_steps: Measure PP1V8_SENSORS and inspect filters
Check for leakage from attached sensor IC
Replace shorted passive if rail is dragged down
Reball sensor hub only after rail is clean
Validate sensor readings

title: Proximity Interrupt Fault
symptoms: Screen stays dark on calls, odd wake behavior, sensor test fails
repair_steps: Inspect upper sensor assembly flex and connector
Measure interrupt line in diode mode
Check pull-up resistor network
Repair open trace or replace connector
Retest proximity and ALS behavior

title: SEP Rail Fault
symptoms: Passcode issues, activation trouble, unusual boot delay
repair_steps: Measure SEP rail for stable 0.9V
Inspect related inductors and capacitors
Check for partial short near secure domain
Replace faulted passive
Retest secure functions

title: USB Authentication Fault
symptoms: Accessory errors, restore disconnects, unstable wired communication
repair_steps: Test with known-good cable and host
Measure auth line and surrounding filters
Inspect port and protection arrays
Replace failed authentication path component
Verify stable data transfer

title: AOP Sensor Wake Fault
symptoms: Intermittent wake, delayed resume, sensor-triggered panic
repair_steps: Measure AOP wake line in diode mode
Inspect resistor packs and sensor wake path
Check always-on rail stability first
Repair open or leaking component on line
Retest sleep-wake behavior

title: Thermal Rail Fault
symptoms: Kernel panic, slow boot, thermal service errors
repair_steps: Check thermal rail and connected sensor lines
Inspect for missing or corroded parts
Compare diode values with donor board
Replace faulty interface or passive
Verify proper thermal reporting

title: NAND Clock Integrity Fault
symptoms: Restore error, logo hang, panic during filesystem mount
repair_steps: Inspect NAND clock resistors and solder state
Measure diode mode and compare with same model
Reball NAND if solder fracture is confirmed
Repair broken pad or via if accessible
Retest restore and full boot

title: PMIC I2C Fault
symptoms: Boot freeze, panic on power management, erratic current pattern
repair_steps: Check SDA and SCL lines for balanced diode values
Inspect pull-up resistor network
Remove shorted client IC if one line is held low
Repair leaking or open bus filter
Retest boot current sequence

title: Filesystem Rail Sag
symptoms: Stuck on logo, restore completes but device will not finish boot
repair_steps: Verify rail presence during late mount stage
Check for output cap ESR or rail droop
Inspect nearby support ICs
Replace unstable source part or capacitor
Retest after full power cycle

title: Secure Storage Data Path Fault
symptoms: Activation errors, logo stall, unstable secure storage access
repair_steps: Inspect interface passives between SEP and storage domain
Measure line set in diode mode for symmetry
Repair damaged filter or series resistor
Rework affected IC only after passive path is verified
Retest activation and storage access

title: ISP Rail Fault
symptoms: Black camera feed, media subsystem instability, boot complete otherwise
repair_steps: Measure ISP rail and inspect local capacitors
Check for short from attached camera line
Disconnect modules one at a time to isolate
Replace shorted component or source IC
Retest all camera functions

title: LiDAR Rail Fault
symptoms: Depth features fail, camera crash, occasional boot lag on Pro models
repair_steps: Inspect LiDAR connector and rail path
Measure diode mode on LiDAR rail
Replace shorted passive or protection part
Swap module if needed to isolate board side
Retest depth camera features

title: Camera MIPI Data Fault
symptoms: Single camera fails, intermittent image, no major hardware panic
repair_steps: Inspect MIPI filter array and connector pins
Check differential pair symmetry in diode mode
Replace damaged filter block
Repair pad damage from previous work if present
Retest affected camera stream

title: Wireless Charging Rail Fault
symptoms: MagSafe or wireless charging unavailable, accessory attach issues
repair_steps: Measure receiver rail in diode mode
Inspect coil connector and magnet assembly contacts
Check receiver IC for heat during wireless power input
Replace damaged receiver IC or input filter
Retest wireless charging

title: UWB Handshake Fault
symptoms: Nearby accessory pairing fails, ecosystem features unstable
repair_steps: Measure interrupt line and inspect pull-up path
Check UWB main rail health first
Repair open trace or cracked resistor
Rework UWB IC only if signal path is intact
Retest accessory discovery

title: USB-C Accessory Detect Fault
symptoms: Accessories not recognized even though charging works
repair_steps: Check accessory detect line and USB-C mux area
Inspect port contamination or wear
Measure diode mode on detect line and mux inputs
Replace damaged mux or ESD array
Retest multiple USB-C accessories

title: USB-C SBU Alt-Mode Fault
symptoms: Dock audio or alternate accessory modes fail intermittently
repair_steps: Measure SBU lines in diode mode and compare symmetry
Inspect switch IC and filter area
Replace damaged switch or protection component
Confirm PD behavior is normal
Retest with supported dock hardware

title: Late UI Rail Instability
symptoms: Logo delay, slow UI load, display subsystem lag
repair_steps: Verify rail stability during late boot
Inspect associated capacitors for leakage
Compare diode value with known-good board
Replace faulty passive on rail
Retest UI load behavior

title: Taptic Boost Fault
symptoms: No vibration on boot, haptic failure, otherwise partial normal startup
repair_steps: Check boost rail and Taptic connector
Inspect for shorted haptic motor
Measure driver output in diode mode
Replace failed driver IC or shorted cap
Retest system haptics

title: Late Sensor Bus Fault
symptoms: UI loads slowly, settings hangs, sensor-related panic after home screen
repair_steps: Check I2C bus for line held low
Isolate attached sensor clients
Replace shorted device-side part
Repair pull-up or corroded filter
Retest full boot and sensor access

title: AOP Resume Request Fault
symptoms: Delayed wake, freeze after lock-unlock cycle, unstable resume behavior
repair_steps: Measure resume request line in diode mode
Inspect resistor ladder and always-on rail source
Repair leakage or open on the signal path
Validate AOP_NMI_POWER first
Retest repeated sleep-wake cycles

title: Final Peripheral Rail Sag
symptoms: Reboots under load, unstable after unlock or app launch
repair_steps: Stress test while monitoring PP3V3_SDR
Inspect for regulator droop or weak cap
Compare diode mode with donor board
Replace unstable regulator or capacitor
Retest with load and charging

title: SYS_ALIVE Return Fault
symptoms: Late boot panic or reboot, often after thermal or charging events
repair_steps: Check SYS_ALIVE and return path passives together
Measure diode values on both handshake lines
Inspect PMIC-side resistor network
Repair signal integrity issue before AP rework
Run repeated cold boot and charger-attached tests
